title: pultenaea subumbellata var. cambagei
intro: Pultenaea subumbellata var. cambagei, also known as Cambage's bushpea, is a plant species in the pea family, Fabaceae, native to southwestern Australia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The medicinal properties of Pultenaea subumbellata var. cambagei are believed to have health benefits such as soothing skin irritations, reducing fever, and aiding in wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve infusing the leaves in water or making a tincture, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human use.
